MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT J. REPPERT, ESQ. AND REPPERT, KELLY & VYTELL, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-Defendants J. Reppert, Esq. and Reppert, Kelly & Vytell, LLC's motion to strike is granted, without leave to amend.

Anson and Johnson Chan are brothers and business partners, and through their companies (East Rex, Yinrex, and Bonds) formed a general partnership (Seaker) to own, develop, operate, lease, and deal with real and personal property in San Francisco (specifically, real property at 135 Post Street). Cross-complainants Seaker, Yinrex, Anson Chan, and Bonds allege that Johnson Chan was able to misappropriate assets, engage in self-dealing behavior, and waste corporate assets with the aid of a company attorney, J. Vincent Reppert. Reppert filed a motion to strike the cross-complaint's prayer for punitive damages, and the Court granted this motion with leave to amend, finding that the cross-complaint did not state specific allegations against Reppert that rose to the level of oppression, fraud, or malice.

A motion to strike punitive damages is properly granted where a plaintiff does not state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, including pleading specific facts showing that a defendant is guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Today's IV, Inc. v. Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (2022) 83 Cal.App.5th 1137, 1193.)

The amended cross-complaint does not state specific allegations against Reppert or RKV that rise to the level of oppression, fraud, or malice. The motion to strike is granted. Having been granted leave to amend once and failing to state more specific allegations against Cross-Defendants, Cross-Complainants are denied leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in complaince with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

Amended MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Nov-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTS J. REPPERT, ESQ., REPPERT, KELLY & VYTELL, LLC'S Amended MOTION TO STRIKE.

Cross-defendants' motion to strike is granted, with leave to amend.

Anson and Johnson Chan are brothers and business partners, and through their companies (East Rex, Yinrex, and Bonds) formed a general partnership (Seaker) to own, develop, operate, lease, and deal with real and personal property in San Francisco (specifically, real property at 135 Post Street). Cross-complainants Seaker, Yinrex, Anson Chan, and Bonds allege that cross-defendant Johnson Chan misappropriated assets, engaged in self-dealing behavior, and wasted corporate assets with the aid of a company attorney, J. Vincent Reppert. Reppert now timely files this motion to strike the cross-complaint's prayer for punitive damages, arguing that the cross-complaint has failed to allege sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages.

A motion to strike punitive damages is properly granted where a plaintiff does not state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, including pleading specific facts showing that a defendant is guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice. (Today's IV, Inc. v. Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (2022) 83 Cal.App.5th 1137, 1193.)

While punitive damages may be appropriate for claims for breach of fiduciary duty and conversion, the cross-complaint does not state specific allegations against Reppert that rise to the level of oppression, fraud, or malice.

Prevailing party to resubmit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances
MATTHEW J. GLUCK, Esq., GLUCK DANIEL ATKINSON LLP. 415-510-2114, litigation@gluckdaniel.com
for Plaintiff EAST REX USA, INC.
Adam S. Cashman. Esq, SINGER CASHMAN LLP,(415) 500-6080 acashman@singercashman.com for Defendant Seaker & Sons; Yinrex USA, Inc.; Anson Chan; and Bonds Land Investment Co., Ltd..
Angela Pourtabib, CSR#13714, 888-707-8366, apourtabib@gmail.com Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

RESPONDENT'S ANSON CHAN, SEAKER & SONS, YINREX USA, INC. and BONDS LAND INVESTMENT CO., LTD.'S Notice Of Defendants' Demurrer.

Defendants' demurrer is overruled. The demurrer states one ground: "that East Rex's first cause of action for dissolution fails to plead facts sufficient to sustain a cause of action." (Not. 1:9-10.) The first amended complaint's first cause of action adequately pleads dissolution under Corporations Code 16801(5). Defendants' notion that a heightened pleading standard applies is not supported by the authority it cites, which involved a trial, not a demurrer. (Bauer v. Bauer (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1106.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR#13714, 888-707-8366, apourtabib@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Defendants' Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
MATTHEW J. GLUCK, Esq., GLUCK DANIEL ATKINSON LLP. 415-510-2114, litigation@gluckdaniel.com
for Plaintiff EAST REX USA, INC.
Adam S. Cashman. Esq, SINGER CASHMAN LLP,(415) 500-6080 acashman@singercashman.com for Defendant Seaker & Sons; Yinrex USA, Inc.; Anson Chan; and Bonds Land Investment Co., Ltd..
Angela Pourtabib, CSR#13714, 888-707-8366, apourtabib@gmail.com Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

RESPONDENT'S ANSON CHAN, SEAKER & SONS, YINREX USA, INC. and BONDS LAND INVESTMENT CO., LTD.'S Motion To Strike.

Defendants' motion to strike is denied. Without demurring to the first amended complaint's second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, defendants seek to strike "numerous allegations made in support of" that claim. (Memo. 6:1-2.) Much of this is useful background information, particularly for the dissolution cause of action. Moreover, "use of the motion to strike should be cautious and sparing," not "a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR#13714, 888-707-8366, apourtabib@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
